title: My Simple Claude Cowork System (for normal people)
summary: Jeff Su presents Claude Cowork, a system that transforms Claude from a chatbot into a full operating system for work. The approach includes CLAUDE.md instruction files, persistent memory, a three-level hierarchy of workstations and projects, and practical demonstrations like Email HQ and spending trackers.
writeup: This video walks through building a persistent, hierarchical workspace for Claude (called CoWork OS) using only plain Markdown files, with no code required. The system centers on two root files: claude.md (the instruction manual governing behavior) and memory.md (a notepad for cross-session recall), organized in a two-level hierarchy analogous to a constitution plus state laws. The through-line is that keeping token usage low while giving Claude persistent, relevant context is achievable through lazy-loading: the root files point to more detailed files that are read only when needed.
key_points: The entire CoWork OS runs on two plain Markdown files: claude.md sets behavioral rules and a routing map to workstation folders, while memory.md stores entries that persist across sessions so Claude can recall projects, preferences, and prior context without re-explanation.

Context is loaded lazily to control token consumption: the root claude.md points to detailed resources files (such as a voice principles file) via references, and Claude only reads those files when the task requires them rather than loading everything at session start.

A two-level hierarchy mirrors a constitution-and-state-law model: root-level rules in claude.md apply to every session, while individual workstation folders (email HQ, personal finances, newsletter HQ) each carry their own claude.md, memory.md, and resources folder with rules that apply only within that workstation.

Voice profiling is automated by feeding Claude a prompt that reads the last 30 sent Gmail messages (or pasted writing samples if Gmail is not connected) and then writes extracted tone and style patterns directly into the voice principles Markdown file, which the newsletter workstation loads before drafting.

Persistent memory is triggered by a natural-language command: telling Claude to 'remember this' causes it to append a structured entry to memory.md, and reading that file at the start of every new session is enforced by a rule inside claude.md itself, making memory self-sustaining without any external database.
